summary: Backend-only testing for face-recognition identity match on punch selfies (Iteration 39). All 27 pytest assertions across 5 test classes passed on the first run. Verified: PATCH /admin/companies/{id}/face-match RBAC (super_admin any / company_admin own / employee 403 / unknown company 404); GET /auth/me returns user.company.face_match_enabled correctly; POST /attendance/punch behaviour under all four toggle+selfie combinations (OFF, ON-no-selfie, ON-selfie-no-photo auto-enrol, ON-selfie-with-photo real Gemini call); punch is NEVER blocked (200 in all cases); GET /admin/attendance/flagged strips selfie_base64/device_info and attaches user_name/employee_code/company_name with proper role scoping; PATCH /admin/attendance/{id}/clear-flag records reviewer + timestamp with cross-company 403; GET /admin/attendance/{id}/selfie and GET /admin/users/{id}/photo return payloads with proper 403/404 gates. Real super_admin PIN fields (pin_hash, pin_must_change, pin_fail_count, pin_locked_until) were snapshotted before tests and re-asserted unchanged at module teardown. Seed data prefixed IT39_ and fully cleaned up.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py at 5,311 lines exceeds the 700-line guideline and should be split (server->routes/services/models modules). Not new for iter39 but worth flagging.

In _compare_faces, the emergentintegrations call has no explicit timeout; a slow Gemini response will hold the punch request open. Consider wrapping chat.send_message in asyncio.wait_for(...) with a bounded timeout (e.g. 20s) so punches never appear to hang for the user.

In list_flagged_punches the enrichment does an unbounded aggregation-in-Python (two extra queries). For companies with hundreds of flagged records this is fine, but consider using $lookup or capping via pipeline for future scale.
identity_confidence is stored as float but the API contract says 0.0-1.0; _compare_faces already clamps, so this is safe today. Documented for future auditors.
seed_data_creation: Seeded 2 companies (cmp_IT39_*_A, cmp_IT39_*_B), 1 super_admin + 2 company_admins + 5 employees, session tokens, and 2 pre-flagged attendance records (att_IT39_*_flagA, att_IT39_*_flagB). All prefixed IT39_ and removed in module teardown. Verified no leftovers in DB after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_iter39_face_match.py as regression. It seeds 2 ephemeral companies and toggles face_match_enabled directly via the endpoint. The 'toggle ON, selfie, with profile photo' test issues ONE real Gemini call (per empA3 and empA4) — cheap because the images are 1x1 JPEGs, but it will consume EMERGENT_LLM_KEY credits. If you need to skip Gemini entirely, either set EMERGENT_LLM_KEY='' (helper returns ok=False, flagged=False) or seed identity_* fields directly via pymongo as done in the flagged_records fixture. Super admin PIN hash is protected by a snapshot-and-restore fixture (sa_pin_snapshot) — never touched.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs found. Implementation matches all 12 acceptance criteria from the review request.
